toolSelection.reason: Selection is strong overall, but one zero-tool-call miss and one clause-coverage/missing-capability miss show the floor is still too low for answerable prompts.

scoutDepth.reason: The prior shallow-discovery bottleneck appears materially improved in this iteration and is no longer the limiting dimension.

clarification.reason: This stage rarely had to do real disambiguation, so the high mean is mostly non-activation rather than strong evidence under ambiguity.

planning.reason: This is the weakest mean score, and the failure class is stable: plans repeatedly under-specify deterministic named-target resolution and clause coverage even when scouting is strong.

execution.reason: A recurring repair-without-retargeting cluster affects a large share of traces, including budget exhaustion and retries that still terminate on the wrong or incomplete target.

synthesis.reason: Synthesis is generally strong; remaining issues mostly inherit from upstream targeting and repair failures rather than synthesis-budget weakness.

contextBudget.reason: Budget use is healthy overall, with only isolated empty-payload or truncated-context outliers.

reasoningDepth.reason: Lower scores are mostly downstream of planning and repair failures rather than a stable standalone reasoning-depth deficit.

mcpTransport.reason: Latency and retry costs still appear in weak traces, but the dominant pattern is upstream mis-targeting rather than transport-only failure.

codeSafety.reason: Validator catches unsafe generic or rejected execution paths reliably, but the repeated warnings point more to planner quality than to a separate safety-system bottleneck.

synthesisBudget.reason: Budget is not a meaningful limiter in this iteration.

interactionRisks.risk: When planning does not deterministically resolve the named target, execution retries tend to stay on the same weak branch and completeness cannot fully recover.
When the planner keeps proposing generic or validator-rejected workflows, repair budget is spent on hygiene and patching instead of progressing toward the grounded answer path.

Clause-partial routing or tool-free completion can leave execution with empty or misaligned canonical payloads, making downstream repair look like an execution problem when the root cause starts earlier.

planning.reason: This is the weakest mean score, and the failure class is stable: plans repeatedly under-specify deterministic named-target resolution and clause coverage even when scouting is strong.

execution.reason: A recurring repair-without-retargeting cluster affects a large share of traces, including budget exhaustion and retries that still terminate on the wrong or incomplete target.
